title: India History and Freedom Movement
description: A deeper Indian history round spanning ancient empires, reform movements, constitutional milestones, and the freedom struggle.

questions.prompt: Which session of the Indian National Congress in 1929 declared Purna Swaraj as the goal?
questions.hint: Jawaharlal Nehru presided over it on the banks of the Ravi.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Servants of India Society in 1905?
questions.hint: He was a moderate leader and mentor to Gandhi.

questions.prompt: Which act transferred power from the East India Company to the British Crown in 1858?
questions.hint: It followed the Revolt of 1857.

questions.prompt: Who was the last Viceroy of India?
questions.hint: He later became independent India's first Governor-General.

questions.prompt: Which newspaper was founded by Bal Gangadhar Tilak in English in 1881?
questions.hint: Its Marathi counterpart was Kesari.

questions.prompt: Which Mughal emperor issued the farman that granted the English East India Company duty-free trade in Bengal in 1717?
questions.hint: He was the great-grandson of Aurangzeb.

questions.prompt: Who led the Bardoli Satyagraha of 1928?
questions.hint: He later became India's Iron Man.

questions.prompt: Which revolutionary organization was founded in 1928 by Bhagat Singh and his comrades after reorganising the HRA?
questions.hint: Its initials are HSRA.

questions.prompt: Which ancient university was destroyed in the late 12th century during Bakhtiyar Khalji's invasion?
questions.hint: It was in present-day Bihar.

questions.prompt: Which governor-general abolished sati in 1829?
questions.hint: He is also linked with English education debates.

questions.prompt: Who authored the nationalist tract Hind Swaraj in 1909?
questions.hint: He wrote it on a sea voyage from London to South Africa.

questions.prompt: Which battle in 1764 firmly established the East India Company's political control in Bengal, Bihar, and Odisha?
questions.hint: It came after Plassey.

questions.prompt: Who was the founder of the Asiatic Society of Bengal?
questions.hint: He was an orientalist judge and scholar.

questions.prompt: Which movement launched by Gandhi in 1930 began with a march from Sabarmati to Dandi?
questions.hint: It challenged the salt tax.

questions.prompt: Which Gupta ruler is associated with the Allahabad Pillar inscription composed by Harisena?
questions.hint: He is often called the Napoleon of India.

questions.prompt: Which commission of 1927 was boycotted because it had no Indian member?
questions.hint: Its arrival led to the slogan 'Go Back'.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Aligarh Movement and the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College?
questions.hint: He was a major Muslim reformer of the 19th century.

questions.prompt: Which Delhi Sultan shifted his capital temporarily from Delhi to Daulatabad?
questions.hint: He was known for ambitious but impractical experiments.

questions.prompt: Which pact in 1932 between Gandhi and B. R. Ambedkar concerned separate electorates for the Depressed Classes?
questions.hint: It was signed in Yerwada Jail context.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Brahmo Samaj in 1828?
questions.hint: He campaigned against sati and for social reform.

questions.prompt: Which Vedic text contains the famous Gayatri Mantra?
questions.hint: It is the oldest of the Vedas.

questions.prompt: Who was the president of the Constituent Assembly of India?
questions.hint: He later became India's first President.

questions.prompt: Which Mughal noble effectively founded the Hyderabad state in the early 18th century?
questions.hint: He was called Asaf Jah I.

questions.prompt: Which act introduced provincial autonomy in British India in 1935?
questions.hint: It was the longest act passed by the British Parliament up to that time.

questions.prompt: Who was the founder of the Satya Shodhak Samaj?
questions.hint: He and Savitribai were pioneers of social reform in Maharashtra.

questions.prompt: Which event of 1905 sparked the Swadeshi Movement in Bengal?
questions.hint: It was a major administrative division by Lord Curzon.

questions.prompt: Which Harappan site in Gujarat is famous for a dockyard?
questions.hint: It lies in the Bhal region.

questions.prompt: Who gave the call 'Do or Die' during the Quit India Movement?
questions.hint: He spoke at the Gowalia Tank Maidan session in August 1942.

questions.prompt: Which Maratha statesman served as Peshwa under Shahu and laid the administrative base of Maratha expansion?
questions.hint: He was the father of Bajirao I.

questions.prompt: Which revolt was led by Titu Mir in Bengal?
questions.hint: It is also remembered for the bamboo fort.
questions.answer: Barasat uprising or Titu Mir's revolt

questions.prompt: Who was the first Indian to become president of the Indian National Congress?
questions.hint: He held the office in 1887.

questions.prompt: Which freedom fighter founded the Ghadar Party in the United States along with others in 1913?
questions.hint: Lala Har Dayal was central to it.

questions.prompt: Which Chola ruler is remembered for naval expeditions to Southeast Asia and building the Brihadisvara Temple's imperial context after his father?
questions.hint: He succeeded Rajaraja I.

questions.prompt: Which agreement between Gandhi and Irwin in 1931 led to the suspension of the Civil Disobedience Movement?
questions.hint: It paved the way for Gandhi to attend the Second Round Table Conference.

questions.prompt: Who wrote Anandamath, the novel that popularized the song Vande Mataram?
questions.hint: He was a Bengali novelist and civil servant.

questions.prompt: Which historian from Kashmir wrote the Rajatarangini?
questions.hint: His name begins with K.

questions.prompt: Which Congress session in 1907 saw the split between moderates and extremists?
questions.hint: It was held in Gujarat.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Forward Bloc in 1939 after resigning from the Congress presidentship?
questions.hint: He later led the INA.

questions.prompt: Which kingdom was ruled by Krishnadevaraya, one of the greatest monarchs of south Indian history?
questions.hint: Its capital was at Hampi.

questions.prompt: Which committee, chaired by Motilal Nehru, produced a constitutional proposal in 1928?
questions.hint: It was the main Indian response before the Round Table era.

questions.prompt: Which medieval bhakti saint from Karnataka composed vachanas and challenged caste orthodoxy?
questions.hint: He is associated with the Lingayat movement.

questions.prompt: Which brothers are among the best-known leaders associated with the Indigo revolt in Bengal?
questions.hint: Their surnames were the same: Biswas.

questions.prompt: Which British proposal of 1946 recommended a federal structure and led to the Constituent Assembly elections?
questions.hint: It was a three-member mission from Britain.

questions.prompt: Who founded the Indian National Army in Southeast Asia before Subhas Chandra Bose took charge?
questions.hint: He was a former officer of the British Indian Army.

questions.prompt: Which battle in 1757 marked the beginning of British political dominance in Bengal?
questions.hint: Mir Jafar's defection was crucial.

questions.prompt: Which social reformer founded the Widow Remarriage Association and strongly advocated women's education in western India?
questions.hint: He was also called Maharshi.

questions.prompt: Which saint-poet of Maharashtra composed abhangs and is closely associated with the Varkari tradition at Pandharpur?
questions.hint: He is often paired with Namdev and Dnyaneshwar.

questions.prompt: Who was the first Law Minister of independent India and chief architect of the Constitution's Drafting Committee?
questions.hint: He was born in Mhow.

questions.prompt: Which uprising of 1855-56 in present-day Jharkhand was led by Sidhu and Kanhu?
questions.hint: It involved an Adivasi community.

questions.prompt: Which emperor's inscriptions at Dhauli and Girnar are major sources for the policy of dhamma?
questions.hint: He converted after the Kalinga war.
